questions.question: What does it mean for an operator S to 'stabilize' a state |ψ⟩?
questions.explanation: A stabilizer S of a state |ψ⟩ means |ψ⟩ is a +1 eigenstate of S: S|ψ⟩ = +|ψ⟩. The state is unchanged by the stabilizer operator.

questions.question: How does an error reveal itself in the stabilizer formalism?

options.text: Errors anticommute with some stabilizers, changing their measurement outcome
Errors cannot be detected in this formalism

questions.explanation: Errors anticommute with some stabilizers. When measured, these stabilizers give eigenvalue -1 instead of +1, revealing the syndrome.

questions.question: What is the code space of a stabilizer code?

options.text: The +1 eigenspace of all stabilizer generators
States that are destroyed by stabilizers

questions.explanation: The code space is the simultaneous +1 eigenspace of all stabilizer generators. States in this space satisfy S|ψ⟩ = |ψ⟩ for all stabilizers S.

questions.question: What defines a logical operator in a stabilizer code?

options.text: An operator that commutes with all stabilizers but is not in the stabilizer group
An operator that anticommutes with all stabilizers

questions.explanation: Logical operators commute with all stabilizers (preserving the code space) but are not themselves stabilizers (so they act non-trivially on encoded information).

questions.question: In an [[n, k, d]] code, how many independent stabilizer generators are there?
questions.explanation: An [[n, k, d]] code has n physical qubits encoding k logical qubits, requiring n-k independent stabilizer generators to define the code space.

questions.question: What is the relationship between code distance d and correctable errors?

options.text: Can correct up to d errors
Cannot correct any errors if d > 1

questions.explanation: A distance-d code can correct up to ⌊(d-1)/2⌋ errors. For example, d=3 can correct 1 error, d=5 can correct 2 errors.

questions.question: The 5-qubit [[5,1,3]] code is the smallest code that can correct any single-qubit error.
questions.explanation: True. The [[5,1,3]] code achieves the quantum Hamming bound and is the smallest possible code that can correct an arbitrary single-qubit error.
